mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, thereby supporting equity valuations and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative on average when FEDFUNDS is below 2% over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate above a critical threshold signals a weakening labor market and potential recessionary pressures, leading to investor pessimism and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp from its 12-month low.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating consumer price inflation combined with strong retail sales indicates an overheating economy. This scenario will likely prompt aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, negatively impacting equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 4% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized change > 5%
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows no significant predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic expansion, strong GDP growth provides a positive backdrop for corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 returns higher. This effect is diminished or reversed during contractions.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative on average during periods when GDPC1 YoY growth is above 2% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector and broader economic weakness, leading to reduced corporate profits and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a 3-month decline in INDPRO greater than 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy labor market and strong consumer demand, which supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative on average during periods of positive PAYEMS growth over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates combined with a significant decline in housing starts indicates that monetary tightening is effectively slowing down the interest-rate sensitive housing sector, signaling broader economic deceleration and negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5% in 6 months AND HOUST 3-month change < -10%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows no significant predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sustained decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer confidence and spending, which is a precursor to reduced corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a 3-month annualized decline in RSAFS greater than 3%.
